description: List clump information. By default, lists clump ID, template name, pebble count, active/total contact count, fixity, and position. Use keywords to list specific attributes, contacts, groups, properties, templates, or results. Use model precision command to control floating point precision. The optional range always applies to clumps and not pebbles.
notes: Use model precision command to change the precision of listed floating point values
The optional range always applies to clumps and not pebbles
Default listing: clump ID, template name, pebble count, active/total contact count, fixity, and position
Attributes are characteristics (mass, velocity) distinct from properties (surface conditions for contact models)

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: Clump getter methods: Clump.pos(), Clump.vel(), Clump.density(), Clump.volume(), etc. - requires iteration: for c in itasca.clump.list(): print(c.pos())

keywords.name: volume
keywords.description: List clump attributes. If no keyword is given, then the available attributes are listed. If the keyword is specified, then the value of that attribute is listed for each clump. Attributes are characteristics of clumps such as mass and velocity. These are distinct from properties that represent surface conditions and are used in contact models to delineate interactions. Clump local damping coefficient. Clump density. Accumulated clump displacement vector as a result of cycling. The \(x\) -component of the accumulated clump displacement as a result of cycling. The \(y\) -component of accumulated displacement as a result of cycling. The \(z\) -component of accumulated displacement as a result of cycling. The \(x\) -euler angle (in degrees) of the current clump orientation. See the euler keyword for further details. The \(y\) -euler angle (in degrees) of the current clump orientation. See the euler keyword for further details. The \(z\) -euler angle (in degrees) of the current clump orientation. See the euler keyword for further details. Force vector applied to clumps. Sum of the contact force accumulated to the clumps during the previous force-displacement update. This value will be modified during the next force-displacement update. The \(x\) -component of the contact force. The \(y\) -component of the contact force. The \(z\) -component of the contact force. Fragment ID (see “ Fragment ”). 11 component of the clump real moment of inertia. When modified, only the clump principal moments of inertia are modified and the other clump attributes are not changed (e.g., volume, pebble sizes). The specification of the clump principal moments of inertia via the command line or via FISH results in the principal moments of inertia being in a fixed state so that they will not be automatically updated when scaling a clump, changing the volume, or changing the density. The fix state can be modified in FISH (see the clump.moi.fix intrinsic). 12 component of the clump real moment of inertia. When modified, only the clump principal moments of inertia are modified and the other clump attributes are not changed (e.g., volume, pebble sizes). The specification of the clump principal moments of inertia via the command line or via FISH results in the principal moments of inertia being in a fixed state so that they will not be automatically updated when scaling a clump, changing the volume, or changing the density. The fix state can be modified in FISH (see the clump.moi.fix intrinsic). 13 component of the clump real moment of inertia. When modified, only the clump principal moments of inertia are modified and the other clump attributes are not changed (e.g., volume, pebble sizes). The specification of the clump principal moments of inertia via the command line or via FISH results in the principal moments of inertia being in a fixed state so that they will not be automatically updated when scaling a clump, changing the volume, or changing the density. The fix state can be modified in FISH (see the clump.moi.fix intrinsic). 22 component of the clump real moment of inertia. When modified, only the clump principal moments of inertia are modified and the other clump attributes are not changed (e.g., volume, pebble sizes). The specification of the clump principal moments of inertia via the command line or via FISH results in the principal moments of inertia being in a fixed state so that they will not be automatically updated when scaling a clump, changing the volume, or changing the density. The fix state can be modified in FISH (see the clump.moi.fix intrinsic). 23 component of the clump real moment of inertia. When modified, only the clump principal moments of inertia are modified and the other clump attributes are not changed (e.g., volume, pebble sizes). The specification of the clump principal moments of inertia via the command line or via FISH results in the principal moments of inertia being in a fixed state so that they will not be automatically updated when scaling a clump, changing the volume, or changing the density. The fix state can be modified in FISH (see the clump.moi.fix intrinsic). 33 component of the clump real moment of inertia. When modified, only the clump principal moments of inertia are modified and the other clump attributes are not changed (e.g., volume, pebble sizes). The specification of the clump principal moments of inertia via the command line or via FISH results in the principal moments of inertia being in a fixed state so that they will not be automatically updated when scaling a clump, changing the volume, or changing the density. The fix state can be modified in FISH (see the clump.moi.fix intrinsic). Real principal moments of inertia. Moment applied to clumps. The \(x\) -component of the moment applied to clumps. The \(y\) -component of the moment applied to clumps. The \(z\) -component of the moment applied to clumps. Sum of the contact moments accumulated to the clumps during the previous force-displacement update. This value will be modified during the next force-displacement update. The \(x\) -component of the contact moment. The \(y\) -component of the contact moment. The \(z\) -component of the contact moment. Location of clump centroid. The \(x\) -component of the location of clump centroids. The \(y\) -component of the location of clump centroids. The \(z\) -component of the location of clump centroids. Current clump orientation. The orientation is updated only when orientation tracking has been enabled (see model orientation-tracking command). Clump angular velocity in radians per second. The \(x\) -component of the clump angular velocity in radians per second. The \(y\) -component of the clump angular velocity in radians per second. The \(z\) -component of the clump angular velocity in radians per second. Clump translational velocity vector. The \(x\) -component of the clump velocity. The \(y\) -component of the clump velocity. The \(z\) -component of the clump velocity. Clump volume. When modified, only the clump mass and real mass are modified and the other clump attributes based on volume are not changed (e.g., moment of inertia, pebble sizes). The specification of the volume via the command line or via FISH results in the principal moments of inertia being in a fixed state so that they will not be automatically updated when scaling a clump, changing the volume, or changing the density. The fixity state of the inertia can be modified in FISH (see the clump.moi.fix intrinsic).

keywords.name: contact
keywords.description: List the contacts with a clump. The pebble ID, contact ID, ID of the other contacting piece, contact type, activity state, inhibited state, and contact model are listed. By default, only active contacts are listed. Use the all keyword to list active and inactive contacts.

keywords.name: extra
keywords.description: List the extra variables at index i . If the pebble keyword is given, then the pebble extra variables at index i are listed.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: List the group names at slot s . If the pebble keyword is given, then the pebble group variables at slot s are listed.

keywords.name: property
keywords.description: List pebble properties. If no s is given, then the available properties are listed for each clump (i.e., the list of properties over all pebbles in the clump). If s is specified, then the value of that property is listed for each pebble. Properties represent surface conditions and are used in contact models to delineate interactions. These are distinct from attributes that are characteristics of balls such as mass and velocity.

keywords.name: result
keywords.description: List the clump model results that have been tabulated. See the clump results command for more details.

keywords.name: template
keywords.description: List information regarding clump templates, including the clump template name, referenced status, pebble count, volume, and density. The attribute kewyord without s lists the available attributes. If s is specified, then the value of that attribute is listed for each clump template.

examples.description: Show the default listing for clumps (ID, template, pebble count, contacts, fixity, position)
List the clump velocities
List all available clump attributes
List value of extra variable at index 2 for all clumps
List value of extra variable at index 1 for all pebbles
List group names at slot 'Default' for all clumps
List group names at slot 'slot1' for all pebbles
List available properties for each clump (properties across all pebbles)
List normal stiffness property for each pebble

examples.command: clump list contact all range id 1 100
examples.description: List all contacts (active and inactive) for clumps 1-100
List all clump templates with name, referenced status, pebble count, volume, and density
List volume attribute for each clump template

keywords.name: attribute
keywords.description: List clump attributes. If no keyword is given, then the available attributes are listed. If a keyword is specified, then the value of that attribute is listed for each clump. Pebble attributes, such as pebble position, also may be present. Attributes are characteristics of clumps such as mass and velocity. These are distinct from properties that represent surface conditions and are used in contact models to delineate interactions.

keywords.name: contact
keywords.description: List the contacts with a clump. The pebble ID, contact ID, ID of the other contacting piece, contact type, activity state, inhibited state, and contact model are listed. By default, only active contacts are listed. Use the all keyword to list active and inactive contacts.

keywords.name: extra
keywords.description: List the extra variables at index i . If the pebble keyword is given, then the pebble extra variables at index i are listed.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: List the group names at slot s . If the pebble keyword is given, then the pebble group variables at slot s are listed.

keywords.name: property
keywords.description: List pebble properties. If no s is given, then the available properties are listed for each clump (i.e., the list of properties over all pebbles in the clump). If s is specified, then the value of that property is listed for each pebble. Properties represent surface conditions and are used in contact models to delineate interactions. These are distinct from attributes that are characteristics of balls such as mass and velocity.

keywords.name: result
keywords.description: List the clump model results that have been tabulated. See the clump results command for more details.

keywords.name: template
keywords.description: List information regarding clump templates, including the clump template name, referenced status, pebble count, volume, and density. The attribute kewyord without s lists the available attributes. If s is specified, then the value of that attribute is listed for each clump template.

examples.description: Show the default listing for clumps (ID, template, pebble count, contacts, fixity, position)
List the clump velocities
List all available clump attributes

List value of extra variable at index 1 for all pebbles
